back_translation: On that day Deborah and Barak son of Abinoam sang this song:
'When the leaders lead in Israel, when the people volunteer, bless the LORD.'
Hear, O kings! Give ear, O princes! I will sing to the LORD; I will sing praise to the LORD, the God of Israel.
O LORD, when You went out from Seir, when You marched from the land of Edom, the earth shook, the heavens poured down rain, even the clouds poured down water.
The mountains shook before the LORD, the One of Sinai, before the LORD, the God of Israel.
In the days of Shamgar son of Anath, in the days of Jael, the highways were deserted, and travelers had to use crooked paths.
Village life ceased — it ended in Israel, until I, Deborah, arose, as a mother in Israel.
When they chose new gods, war came to the gates. There was no shield or spear among forty thousand men in Israel.
My heart is with the leaders of Israel, with the volunteers among the people. Bless the LORD!
You who ride white donkeys, you who sit on saddle blankets, and you who travel on the road, ponder this!

Listen to the voices of singers at the watering places. There they will recount the righteous deeds of the LORD, the righteous deeds of the villagers in Israel. Then the people of the LORD will go down to the gates.
'Awake, awake, Deborah! Awake, awake, sing a song! Arise, Barak, lead away your captives, son of Abinoam!'
Then the survivors came down to the leaders; the people of the LORD came down to me against the mighty.

People from Ephraim also came, those with their roots in Amalek; following you, Benjamin came with your people. The commanders came down from Machir, and those who carry the leader's staff came from Zebulun.
The princes of Issachar were with Deborah, and Issachar was with Barak, rushing into the valley after him. In the divisions of Reuben there was great searching of heart.
Why did you sit among the sheepfolds, listening to the whistling for the flocks? In the divisions of Reuben there was great searching of heart.
Gilead remained beyond the Jordan, and Dan — why did he remain by the ships? Asher stayed at the seacoast and remained in his harbors.
Zebulun was a people who risked their lives, and Naphtali also, on the heights of the battlefield.
Kings came and fought, then the kings of Canaan fought at Taanach by the waters of Megiddo, but they took no silver as plunder.
From the heavens the stars fought — from their courses they fought against Sisera.
The River Kishon swept them away — the ancient river, the River Kishon. March on, O my soul, in strength!
Then the hooves of horses struck the ground — the galloping, the galloping of his stallions.
'Curse Meroz,' said the angel of the LORD. 'Curse her inhabitants severely, because they did not come to help the LORD, did not come to help the LORD fight against the mighty.'
Jael wife of Heber the Kenite is most blessed among women — most blessed among tent-dwelling women.
He asked for water, she gave him milk; in a magnificent bowl she brought him curds.
She reached out her hand for the tent peg, her right hand for the workman's hammer. She struck Sisera, she crushed his head; she shattered and pierced his temple.
At her feet he sank down, he fell, he lay there; at her feet he sank down, he fell; where he sank down, there he fell dead.
Sisera's mother looked out through the window; she peered through the lattice and wailed: 'Why is his chariot so long in coming? Why are the wheels of his chariots delayed?'
Her wisest court ladies answered, and she answered herself,

'They must be finding and dividing the spoil — a girl or two for each warrior — dyed cloth as spoil for Sisera, embroidered dyed cloth as spoil — two pieces of embroidered dyed cloth for the necks of the plunderers.'
So may all Your enemies perish, O LORD! But may those who love You shine like the sun when it rises in its strength.' Then the land had rest from war for forty years.
